summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of Northcrest Mortgage Lead Gen app. Backend: 21/21 pytest tests pass (public endpoints, calculator, leads, subscribers (incl duplicate), mocked chat, auth gating 401, synthetic-session-based admin endpoints CRUD/analytics/CSV export). Frontend: hero renders correct headline, 6-step calculator flow with sliders/credit buttons/state select/lead capture works, validation blocks invalid email, submit POSTs to /api/leads and shows ResultView with home price range, monthly payment, and program badges. Lead magnet success state shows. FAQ accordion expands. Admin /login button correctly navigates to https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=... with proper /admin redirect param. Admin dashboard with synthetic session cookie loads metric cards, lead table, search, status dropdown, Notes save (Notes saved toast), CSV export button visible. ONE confirmed UI bug: floating chatbot toggle is overlapped by the Emergent badge anchor (#emergent-badge) at bottom-right; pointer events intercepted so user clicks never reach the chat button (JS-dispatched click works, confirming React state is fine).

ui_bugs.issue: Floating chatbot toggle button at bottom-right is overlapped by the Emergent badge (#emergent-badge) anchor; document.elementFromPoint at the button center returns the badge. Real user clicks are intercepted and the chat window does not open. React onClick handler itself works (JS .click() opens window and mocked replies render correctly).
ui_bugs.suggested_fix: Move chatbot toggle to a different position (e.g. bottom-left, or raise it ~bottom-24 right-6) so it does not collide with the Emergent badge at bottom-right. Increasing z-index alone will NOT help because the badge is also z-50+ and rendered above; physical repositioning is required.

action_items: Reposition the ChatBot floating button so it no longer collides with the Emergent 'Made with Emergent' badge at bottom-right (e.g. move to bottom-left, or to bottom-24 right-6 — above the badge). Required for the chatbot feature to be usable by end users.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses os.environ['MONGO_URL'] and os.environ['DB_NAME'] directly (no default) — good, fails fast as required.
All Mongo queries use {'_id': 0} projection — good.
/api/auth/session sets cookie with samesite=none and secure=true — correct for cross-origin admin flow.
Calculator.jsx is 361 lines — borderline but acceptable; consider extracting ResultView and step components if it grows.
AdminLogin.jsx correctly avoids hardcoded fallbacks and uses window.location.origin + '/admin' as the redirect — verified live navigation works.
All mocked integrations (email/SMS/CRM, chat) are clearly logged via logger.info with [MOCK ...] prefixes — easy to spot in logs.
test_credentials: No real credentials needed. Synthetic admin session created in MongoDB via mongosh per /app/auth_testing.md; cleaned up after tests.
seed_data_creation: Test lead created via UI calculator flow and one via API (both prefixed TEST_, cleaned up). Test subscriber email also created & cleaned. Synthetic admin user/session created in MongoDB then deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py — run with MONGO_URL/DB_NAME/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env vars (all default-friendly). Synthetic session helper pattern: insert into users + user_sessions collections with matching user_id, then send session_token either as cookie or Bearer header. The ONLY pending fix is repositioning the ChatBot floating button to avoid collision with #emergent-badge. After fix, re-test by clicking chatbot-toggle with normal (non-force) Playwright click and confirming chatbot-window appears.
rca of the issue: Bug RCA: ChatBot.jsx (line 43-50) places the toggle button at 'fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-50'. The Emergent platform injects an anchor with id 'emergent-badge' at the same coordinate range (bottom-right corner) with a higher effective stacking. document.elementFromPoint at the button's center returns the badge <a>, so click events bubble to the badge and open the Emergent site (target=_blank) instead of toggling the chat. Mitigation: move the button to a non-conflicting corner (e.g. bottom-6 left-6) or to bottom-24 right-6 so it sits above the badge.
